mnemonics: FCN: Fully Connected Networks are the Foundation, Core, and Nucleus of deep learning.
CNN: Cats Never Nap. They use Convolutional Neural Networks to spot mice (features) in pictures.
RNN: Recalling Neural Narratives. They Remember what was said Next to predict what comes next.
keyPoints: Feedforward Neural Networks (FNNs) are the basic architecture where information flows in one direction from input to output.
Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) use convolutional layers to efficiently process spatial data like images by detecting hierarchical patterns.
Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) are designed for sequential data by having internal loops, allowing them to persist information across time steps.
commonMistakes: Using a standard FNN for image data, which ignores spatial locality and requires an intractable number of parameters.
Applying an RNN to non-sequential, independent data, which adds unnecessary complexity and training difficulty.
Forgetting to use mechanisms like LSTM or GRU to combat the vanishing gradient problem in simple RNNs for long sequences.
